title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 September 2024.

items.title: iOS versions of **Resident Evil 4, 7** and **Village** now need an online connection at startup
bullets: The update has left some players unable to play the games on the move

items.title: Helldivers 2 and Dead Island 2 support studio **Toadman ** Interactive confirms layoffs
bullets: Toadman is owned by Enad Global 7 (EG7) and has worked on notable releases like Helldivers 2, Dead Island 2, Killing Floor Calamity, and Warhammer: End times–Vermintide.

"The annual cost savings for Toadman is approximately SEK 63 million ($6.1 million) and will affect approximately 100 full-time and contract resources [headcount] previously engaged with Toadman," reads the report.
Some positions at the Immortal Unchained developer's Berlin office are also at risk.

items.title: Thomas Koegler appointed CEO of **Asmodee**
bullets: Former deputy COO will also join Embracer's executive management team

Kogler joined the French board game publisher in 2015 where he held various roles, most recently as the company's deputy COO. He will also replace Stéphane Carville on Embracer's executive management team.

items.title: **Rocksteady** hit by layoffs after **Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League** underperforms
bullets: One staff member made redundant while on paternity leave
There has also been an acknowledgement by Rocksteady's senior management that product quality will now suffer as a result, staff say.
Staff members affected by the job cuts include numerous junior staff, but also several team members employed at Rocksteady for more than five years.

items.title: **Nintendo of Europe** president steps down after six years
bullets: Stephan Bole has been replaced by Luciano Pereña, who is now the company's CEO, president and chairman
